title: Chip Stocks Poised for Bear Market in AI Unwind: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in chipmakers gathered pace on Friday, driving the group that has led this year’s stock rally toward a bear market on worries that the artificial-intelligence spending spree is becoming harder to justify.

title: Gold Heads for Second Weekly Drop as War Raises Fed Hike Fears
summary: Gold was on track for the biggest weekly loss since early June as renewed hostilities in the Middle East raised concerns that higher interest rates will be needed to contain war-driven inflation.

title: Anglo Has Chosen a Preferred Bidder for De Beers, Botswana Says
summary: Anglo American Plc has chosen a preferred bidder for its sale of De Beers, a Botswanan cabinet minister said.

title: BASF Seeks Banks to Lead IPO of €20 Billion Agrichemical Unit
summary: BASF SE has invited banks to pitch for roles on an initial public offering of its agrichemical division in Frankfurt, in what could be one of the largest offerings the bourse has seen in recent years.

title: US Stocks Plunge as Concerns of ‘DeepSeek 2.0’ Hit Chipmakers
summary: US stocks plunged on Friday as the selloff in semiconductor names gathered steam following the reveal of an artificial-intelligence model from a Chinese startup.

title: Hyperscalers Drag Down Bond Gauges Across Global Markets
summary: The bonds sold by hyperscalers to fuel their artificial intelligence ambitions have become a drag on investor portfolios from London to Tokyo.

title: Neglected Quant Safety Trade Makes a Comeback as AI Risks Rise
summary: As worries build around the artificial intelligence trade, investors are starting to seek shelter in a more pedestrian corner of the stock market: companies with sturdy finances.

title: IBM’s Warning Is a ‘Hammer’ Slamming Down on Tech’s AI Outsiders
summary: International Business Machines Corp.’s warning this week that its sales are falling well short of expectations highlights a growing divide between artificial intelligence winners and everyone else in the tech universe.

title: Apple Tops Nvidia as World’s Largest Company Amid Tech Rotation
summary: Apple Inc. is again the biggest company in the world after wresting the title from Nvidia Corp., which has held it since May 2025.

title: Chips Stocks Tumble Into Bear Market as 105% AI Rally Fizzles
summary: A closely-watched index of semiconductor stocks has fallen into a bear market, unwinding a chunk of the blistering memory-led rally that saw the gauge more than double in just three months.

title: Hedge Fund Symmetry Gets Regulatory Nod to Operate in Dubai
summary: Symmetry Investments has received the regulatory approval to operate in Dubai, joining hedge fund peers who’re continuing to expand in the emirate despite the regional conflict.

title: Bank of Italy Raises Growth Forecast in Another Boon for Meloni
summary: The Bank of Italy raised its outlook for the country’s economy, judging that a positive start to the year will outweigh fallout that followed from the Iran war.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
summary: Oil was heading for its biggest weekly advance since April on mounting signs that the Middle East conflict is escalating, threatening flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Apple Upgraded to Buy by HSBC on Agentic AI, Hardware Pipeline
summary: Apple Inc. was upgraded to buy at HSBC Holdings Plc on Friday, in the latest reflection of how Wall Street is increasingly positive on the iPhone maker’s position in a market where many trades related to artificial intelligence have been faltering.

title: Coronation Trims TSMC, SK Hynix on AI Hype, Boosts India Stocks
summary: Coronation Asset Management Ltd., which oversees $47 billion, has trimmed its exposure to chipmakers and increased its allocation to India, as expectations for artificial intelligence stocks have risen to levels that are nearly impossible to beat.

title: Oil Market Can’t Live Without Hormuz Barrels for Long
summary: Flows through the critical waterway haven’t fully stopped, but signs of stress in the market are mounting.

title: Innolight Gets Approval for Up to $8 Billion Hong Kong Listing
summary: Zhongji Innolight Co. received approval from China’s securities regulator and the Hong Kong stock exchange for what is likely to be one of the city’s biggest listings in years.

title: Reliance Founders Lift Stake by Most Since 2019 as Stock Slumps
summary: Reliance Industries Ltd. founders, led by billionaire Mukesh Ambani, raised stake in the Indian oil-to-telecom conglomerate by the most in nearly seven years following the stock’s prolonged slump in the wake of the US-Iran war.

title: Lula’s Panda-Bond Push Tests China’s Appeal for Brazil’s Firms
summary: Brazil has big ambitions for its plan to enter China’s onshore bond market — if only corporate Brazil follows along.

title: Amundi Says Oil, Gas Crucial to EU’s Clean-Energy Transition
summary: Amundi SA wants the European Union to free asset managers to add oil and gas exposures to a new fund category intended to support the transition to a lower-carbon economy.
